$comment: Coverage matrix for the Carve TextMate grammar (syntaxes/carve.tmLanguage.json), driven by the shared markup-carve/carve corpus (spec/tests/corpus/*.crv). Every corpus category must appear in exactly one of `covered` or `skip`. The coverage test (tests/coverage.test.mjs) fails if a category is in neither, so a new spec category forces a deliberate decision. `covered` maps a category prefix to a representative corpus file that is tokenized and snapshotted. `skip` maps a category prefix to the reason TextMate highlighting legitimately does not produce a distinct, snapshot-worthy scope for it.

skip.20-table-column-alignment: Column alignment is encoded in the separator-row colons; the grammar scopes the separator as a table header/separator token but does not distinguish per-column alignment, which is a render-time concern, not a highlighting distinction.
skip.21-table-per-cell-alignment-override: Per-cell alignment markers are not separately scoped from ordinary cell punctuation; alignment is a layout concern with no distinct token.
skip.22-headerless-table-alignment: Same cell/separator tokens as a normal table; headerless-ness is structural, not a scope difference.
skip.23-table-without-alignment: Produces the same cell/separator tokens as any table; the absence of alignment markers yields no distinct scope.
skip.24-table-alignment-with-colspan: Colspan markers are covered by 10-tables-with-rowspan-and-colspan; the alignment interaction adds no new scope.
skip.25-table-doubled-alignment-marker: A doubled alignment marker is a parser edge case for layout; it produces no distinct highlighting token.
skip.27-blockquote-caption-after-a-blank-line: Caption association across a blank line is a block-structure/parse concern; the caption and quote tokens themselves are covered by 07 and 09.
skip.28-table-cell-escaped-pipe: Whether an escaped pipe ends a cell is parser-level; the escape token itself is covered by 60-escapes and cell punctuation by 09-tables.
skip.29-table-cell-pipe-inside-code-span: A pipe inside an inline code span is a parse-boundary concern; code-span and table tokens are covered by 12 and 09.
skip.30-abbreviation-matches-on-word-boundari: Abbreviation usage in prose is not highlighted at all (only the definition is, covered by 14); word-boundary matching is a resolver concern with no token.
skip.31-mention-ignores-email-addresses: Negative case: an email address must NOT become a mention. There is no positive scope to snapshot; the mention regex boundary is exercised by 15 and 89.
skip.32-tag-requires-a-word-boundary: Negative/boundary case for tag detection; no distinct positive scope beyond what 15-mentions-and-tags already covers.
skip.33-table-stacked-rowspan: Stacked rowspan is a layout/parse concern; the rowspan marker token is covered by 10.
skip.35-collapsed-reference-link: A collapsed reference link tokenizes the same as a reference link (covered by 34); collapsing is a resolver concern.
skip.36-unresolved-reference-link: An unresolved reference link still tokenizes as a reference link; resolution status is not a highlighting distinction.
skip.39-smart-typography-escapes-and-code: Asserts that escapes and code spans suppress smart-typography; the escape and code tokens are covered by 60 and 12, and the suppressed sequences have no positive scope.
skip.40-table-multi-line-cell-continuation: Multi-line cell continuation is block structure; the continuation marker is covered by 100/83 and table cells by 09.
skip.41-table-rowspan-with-multi-line-content: Combines rowspan (covered by 10) with multi-line content (structural); no new distinct token.
skip.48-hard-line-breaks: A trailing backslash hard break is not given a distinct scope by the grammar; it is a render-time line-break concern.
skip.49-non-breaking-space: A backslash-space non-breaking space is a render-time concern; the grammar does not scope it distinctly.
skip.54-ordered-marker-vs-prose: Negative case distinguishing a real ordered marker from prose like '1985.'; marker tokens are covered by 52/53 and the prose case has no marker scope.
skip.55-footnote-with-multiple-blocks: Multi-block footnote bodies are block structure; the footnote reference/definition tokens are covered by 43.
skip.57-thematic-breaks: Thematic breaks (---, ***, ___) are not given a dedicated scope by the grammar; they are block-structure separators (and overlap visually with emphasis/typography runs).
skip.61-empty-delimiters: Empty delimiter runs deliberately produce no emphasis token; there is no positive scope to assert.
skip.62-bare-urls-stay-literal: Bare URLs are intentionally left as plain text with no scope; the point of the category is the absence of a link token.
skip.63-nested-containers: Container nesting is block structure; the div/quote/list tokens are each covered by 44/07/05.
skip.64-attribute-edge-cases: Attribute parsing edge cases; the attribute tokens themselves are covered by 17 and 75-style cases, with no new scope per edge.
skip.65-escape-coverage: Exhaustive escape coverage is a parser concern; the escape token is covered by 60-escapes.
skip.68-parenthesized-ordered-marker: Carve does not treat parenthesized (1) as an ordered marker, so there is no marker token to snapshot (negative case).
skip.69-emphasis-edge-cases: Emphasis edge cases (delimiter runs, flanking) are covered representatively by 01-emphasis, 71, and 74; per-edge structural variants add no new scope.
skip.70-list-nesting-and-looseness: List looseness/nesting is block structure; bullet and ordered-marker tokens are covered by 05/52/53.
skip.71-doubled-emphasis-delimiters: Doubled-delimiter handling resolves to the same emphasis/strong tokens covered by 01-emphasis.
skip.72-nested-brackets-in-link-text: Nested brackets in link text is a link-parsing edge; the link tokens are covered by 03/34.
skip.73-reference-labels-are-case-sensitive: Case sensitivity of reference labels is a resolver concern; the reference tokens are covered by 34.
skip.74-two-char-delimiter-runs: Two-character delimiter runs resolve to the same emphasis tokens covered by 01-emphasis.
skip.75-trailing-attribute-block-edge-cases: Trailing attribute-block edges resolve to the attribute tokens covered by 17 and 84.
skip.76-paragraph-interruption: Whether a construct interrupts a paragraph is block-structure parsing; the interrupting constructs are each covered by their own category.
skip.77-blockquote-lazy-continuation: Lazy continuation is block-structure parsing; the blockquote token is covered by 07.
skip.79-multi-line-headings: Carve heading markers are single-line/column-0 (see 101); multi-line heading association is structural with no extra token.
skip.80-blockquote-lazy-continuation-stops-at: Lazy-continuation termination at a fence is block-structure parsing; quote and fence tokens are covered by 07 and 11.
skip.81-list-lazy-continuation: List lazy continuation is block-structure parsing; list tokens are covered by 05.
skip.82-compact-list-blocks: List compactness/looseness is block structure with no distinct token; bullets covered by 05.
skip.88-line-blocks: A line block (::: | ... :::) tokenizes via the div opener/closer already covered by 44-generic-divs; line-block semantics are render-time.
skip.89-mention-and-tag-name-boundaries: Name-boundary acceptance for mentions/tags is exercised by the mention/tag regex already covered by 15; the boundary edges are negative cases with no new positive scope.
skip.92-strong-emphasis-starting-with-a-link: Interaction of strong emphasis with a leading link resolves to emphasis and link tokens covered by 01 and 03.
skip.93-abbreviation-definition-interrupts-a-: Whether an abbreviation definition interrupts a paragraph is block-structure parsing; the definition token is covered by 14.
skip.94-literal-less-than-in-prose: A literal less-than in prose deliberately produces no autolink token; the point is the absence of a scope.
skip.102-paragraph-trailing-whitespace: Trailing-whitespace stripping is a parser/render concern with no token-scope difference (plain paragraph text).
skip.104-blocked-span-marker-renders-as-empty: How a blocked span marker resolves to an empty cell is table-layout resolution; the '^'/'<' marker tokens are covered by 10-tables-with-rowspan-and-colspan and 96.
skip.105-colspan-marker-scans-left-past-a-con: Left-scanning of a colspan marker across consumed cells is table-layout resolution; the marker tokens are covered by 10 and 96.
skip.106-security-hardening: Scheme and attribute sanitization (javascript:, ms-msdt:, onclick=, style=) happens on HTML output. A rejected URL or attribute tokenizes exactly like an accepted one, so there is no highlighting distinction; link/attribute tokens are covered by 03 and 17.
skip.107-link-destination-stops-at-the-first-: Where the destination ends is link-parsing; the link tokens themselves are covered by 03-links (and the bracket-nesting edge by 72).
skip.109-cross-references-resolve-inside-foot: Cross-reference resolution scope is a resolver concern; the crossref and footnote tokens are covered by 58 and 43.
skip.111-a-pipe-pair-with-no-cell-is-not-a-ta: Negative case: '||' must not become a table. TextMate matches on the line shape alone and has no positive scope to assert here; table tokens are covered by 09.
skip.113-a-continuation-row-needs-a-body-row: Negative/structural case for table continuation rows; the '+' continuation marker token is covered by the table-continuation pattern exercised in 40/09.
skip.115-footnote-definition-inside-a-contain: Collecting a footnote definition out of a blockquote or list is a resolver concern; the quote/list and footnote-definition tokens are covered by 07, 05 and 43.
skip.116-cyclic-cross-reference-resolves-to-o: Cycle-breaking during cross-reference resolution is a render-time concern; the crossref token is covered by 58.
skip.117-trojan-source-heading-ids-are-nfc-no: NFC normalization and invisible-control stripping affect generated ids and output text, not tokens; the heading tokens are covered by 02 and 19.
skip.118-trojan-source-rendered-text-and-code: Bidi-override stripping is an output-sanitization concern; the surrounding text/code-span tokens are covered by 12-inline-code.
skip.119-scheme-probe-strips-unicode-whitespa: Unicode-whitespace stripping before the scheme probe is URL sanitization on output; the reference-definition token is covered by 34.
skip.121-classes-are-deduplicated: Class deduplication happens when emitting the class attribute; both duplicates tokenize identically as class names, which are covered by 17-attributes.
skip.123-a-bare-attribute-block-on-its-own-li: Negative case: '{}' stays literal. The attribute-block pattern requires at least one character, so there is no token to assert; block attribute lines are covered by 84.
skip.124-a-backslash-in-a-link-destination-is: How a backslash inside a destination is interpreted is link parsing; the link tokens are covered by 03 and the escape token by 60.
skip.125-autolink-display-keeps-the-raw-conte: Keeping the raw autolink content in the display text is a render concern; the autolink tokens (scheme and email forms) are covered by 59-autolinks.
